text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #20 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Martin Maechler from comment #13)
> Created attachment 3665
> "Test code R script"  (MM)

On '[<-.POSIXlt' and '[[<-.POSIXlt':


In
x[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
and
x.[[i]] <- x25[[i]]
, 'x', 'x.' and 'x25' have identical 'tzone' attributes.
There is no difference from R 4.5.3, direct components subassignments is done.

x[1:25] - x25[1:25]
all 0

x$sec == x25$sec
all TRUE

x$wday == x25$wday
all TRUE

x.[[7]] - x25[[7]]
0

x.[[25]] - x25[[25]]
0

x.$sec[c(7, 25)] == x25$sec[c(7, 25)]
c(TRUE, TRUE)

x.$wday[c(7, 25)] == x25$wday
c(TRUE, TRUE)


Suppose that

lcx. <- lcx
lcx[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
lcx.[[7]] <- x25[[7]]
lcx.[[25]] <- x25[[25]]

is added.
The 'tzone' attribute of 'x25' has the same first element as, but different
length from the 'tzone' attribute of 'lcx' or 'lcx.', except when 'thtz' is
"UTC".
R 4.5.3: direct components subassignments
R 4.6.0:
- thtz="UTC": direct components subassignments
- else: as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = *) first
r89926: direct components subassignments

lcx[1:25] - x25[1:25]
all 0

lcx$sec == x25$sec
R 4.5.3: all TRUE
R 4.6.0:
- thtz="UTC": all TRUE
- else:
i=1 to i=5: FALSE from 8
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE
r89926: all TRUE

lcx$wday == x25$wday
R 4.5.3: all TRUE
R 4.6.0:
- thtz="UTC": all TRUE
- else:
i=1: FALSE from 8
i=2: all FALSE
i=3 to i=4: TRUE from 8
i=5: all FALSE
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE
r89926: all TRUE

lcx.[[7]] - x25[[7]]
0

lcx.[[25]] - x25[[25]]
0

lcx.$sec[c(7, 25)] == x25$sec[c(7, 25)]
R 4.5.3: TRUE TRUE
R 4.6.0:
- thtz="UTC": TRUE TRUE
- else:
i=1 to i=5: TRUE FALSE
i=6 to i=11: FALSE FALSE
r89926: TRUE TRUE

lcx.$wday[c(7, 25)] == x25$wday
R 4.5.3: TRUE TRUE
R 4.6.0:
- thtz="UTC": TRUE TRUE
- else:
i=1: TRUE FALSE
i=2: FALSE FALSE
i=3 to i=4: FALSE TRUE
i=5 to i=11: FALSE FALSE
r89926: TRUE TRUE


Suppose that

xU. <- xU <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(25), tz = "Etc/UTC"))
xU[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
xU.[[7]] <- x25[[7]]
xU.[[25]] <- x25[[25]]

is added.
The 'tzone' attribute of 'x25' has different first element from the 'tzone'
attribute of 'xU' or 'xU.'. The 'tzone' attribute of 'xU' or 'xU.' has length
3.
R 4.5.3: direct components subassignments
R 4.6.0: as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = *) first
r89926:
[<- : as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = *) then correct sec first
[[<- : as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = attr(*, "tzone")) then correct sec first,
*error*

xU[1:25] - x25[1:25]
R 4.5.3:
- thtz="UTC": all 0
- else: all not 0
R 4.6.0: all 0
r89926:
i=1 to i=5: 1 sec at 8 to 23
i=6: 1 sec at 7 to 23
i=7: 1 sec at 6 to 23
i=8: 1 sec at 5 to 23
i=9: 1 sec at 3 to 23
i=10: 1 sec at 1 to 23
i=11: all 0
*floor sec before as.POSIXct: all 0

xU$sec == x25$sec
R 4.5.3: all TRUE
R 4.6.0, r89926:
i=1 to i=5: FALSE from 8
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE
*floor sec before as.POSIXct:
i=1 to i=10: FALSE from 24
i=11: all FALSE

xU$wday == x25$wday
R 4.5.3: all TRUE
R 4.6.0, r89926:
- thtz="UTC":
i=1: FALSE from 8
i=2: all FALSE
i=3 to i=4: TRUE from 8
i=5: all FALSE
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE
- thtz="Europe/Kyiv","Asia/Hebron","Asia/Kolkata","Pacific/Auckland":
i=1: all TRUE
i=2 to i=5: all FALSE
i=6 to i=11: all TRUE
- thtz="Canada/Mountain","Navajo":
i=1 to i=2: all FALSE
i=3 to i=4: all TRUE
i=5 to i=11: all FALSE
*floor sec before as.POSIXct:
- thtz="UTC":
i=1: FALSE from 24
i=2: all FALSE
i=3 to i=4: TRUE from 24

elements.text: [Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy

(In reply to Martin Maechler from comment #13)

On '[<-.POSIXlt' and '[[<-.POSIXlt':



In
x[1:25] <- x25[1:25]
and
x.[[i]] <- x25[[i]]
, 'x', 'x.' and 'x25' have identical 'tzone' attributes.
There is no difference from R 4.5.3, direct components subassignments is done.

all 0

length from the 'tzone' attribute of 'lcx' or 'lcx.', except when 'thtz' is
"UTC".

Addition, modified from example in my E-mail to R-devel mailing list about
subassigning a POSIXlt object without 'tzone' attribute,
<https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-devel/2026-April/084489.html> :


x1 <- as.POSIXlt(Sys.time()); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL; x2 <- x1
x1[1L] <- y <- Sys.time()
x2[[1L]] <- y

R 4.5.3: as.POSIXlt(*)
R 4.6.0: as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = NULL), *error*
r89926:
[<- : as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = "")
[[<- : as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = NULL), *error*

x1[1L] - y
R 4.5.3: 0
r89926: 0

x2[[1L]] - y
R 4.5.3: 0


x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(2))); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL
x2 <- x1
y <- list(as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "Etc/UTC")),
          as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(0, tz = "Pacific/Auckland")))
x1[1L] <- y[[1L]]
x1[2L] <- y[[2L]]
x2[[1L]] <- y[[1L]]
x2[[2L]] <- y[[2L]]

R 4.5.3: direct components subassignment
R 4.6.0: as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = NULL) first
r89926:
[<- : as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = "") then correct sec first
[[<- : as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = NULL) then correct sec first

c(x1[1L] - y[[1L]], x1[2L] - y[[2L]])
R 4.5.3, R 4.6.0: not all 0
r89926: all 0

c(identical(c(unclass(x1[1L])), c(unclass(y[[1L]]))),
  identical(c(unclass(x1[2L])), c(unclass(y[[2L]]))))
# c(unclass(*)) removes attributes other than names
R 4.5.3, R 4.6.0: all TRUE
r89926: not all TRUE

c(x2[[1L]] - y[[1L]], x2[[2L]] - y[[2L]])
R 4.5.3, R 4.6.0, r89926: not all 0
*as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = tz1(x)): all 0

c(identical(c(unclass(x2[[1L]])), c(unclass(y[[1L]]))),
  identical(c(unclass(x2[[2L]])), c(unclass(y[[2L]]))))
R 4.5.3, R 4.6.0, r89926: all TRUE
*as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = tz1(x)): not all TRUE


Addition:


x1 <- as.POSIXlt(.POSIXct(numeric(2))); attr(x1, "tzone") <- NULL
x2 <- x1
x1[1:2] <- y <- c("2006-02-20 11:16:16.683000", "9999-12-31 23:59:59.999999")
x2[[1L]] <- y[[1L]]; x2[[2L]] <- y[[2L]]

R 4.5.3: as.POSIXlt(*)
R 4.6.0: as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = NULL)
r89926:
[<- :
- attr(as.POSIXlt(*), "tzone")[1] is "": as.POSIXlt(*)
- else: as.POSIXlt(*) then as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = "") then correct sec
[[<- : as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(*), tz = NULL)

x1[1:2] - y
error

x1[1:2] == y
R 4.5.3, R 4.6.0: TRUE TRUE
r89926:
- attr(as.POSIXlt(y), "tzone")[1] is "": TRUE TRUE
- else: TRUE FALSE
*floor sec before as.POSIXct: TRUE TRUE

identical(format(x1, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%OS6"), y)
R 4.5.3: TRUE
R 4.6.0: FALSE
r89926:
- attr(as.POSIXlt(y), "tzone")[1] is "": TRUE

x1[1:2] <- y <- c("2006-02-20 11:16:16.683000", "9999-12-31 23:59:59.999999")

error

- else: TRUE FALSE

- else: FALSE

text:
*[Bug 19058] New: abbreviate() can give unexpected output with non-ASCII input*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19058>

            Bug ID: 19058
           Summary: abbreviate() can give unexpected output with non-ASCII
                    input
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Other
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3681
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3681&action=edit>
Use iswlower() rather than islower() when determining if a wide character is
lowercase in the current locale.

This is perhaps expected given the warning, but given that this is an issue
that could be resolved, here's an example:

```
> abbreviate("přístřešek")
přístřešek
    "přřš"
Warning message:
In abbreviate("přístřešek") : abbreviate used with non-ASCII chars

> R.version
               _
platform       aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
arch           aarch64
os             linux-gnu
system         aarch64, linux-gnu
status         Under development (unstable)
major          4
minor          7.0
year           2026
month          04
day            23
svn rev        89955
language       R
version.string R Under development (unstable) (2026-04-23 r89955)
nickname       Unsuffered Consequences
```

Compare with a similar build of R on macOS, and note the difference in behavior
-- likely owing to different behavior in `islower()` between the two different
libc implementations.

```
> abbreviate("přístřešek")
Warning in abbreviate("přístřešek") :
  abbreviate used with non-ASCII chars
přístřešek
    "přst"
>
> R.version
               _
platform       aarch64-apple-darwin25.4.0
arch           aarch64
os             darwin25.4.0
system         aarch64, darwin25.4.0
status         Under development (unstable)
major          4
minor          7.0
year           2026
month          04
day            23
svn rev        89955
language       R
version.string R Under development (unstable) (2026-04-23 r89955)
nickname       Unsuffered Consequences
```

This comes down to the use of `islower()` rather than `iswlower()` in
`wstripchars`. A simple patch is attached.

text:
*[Bug 19059] New: as.data.frame.vector() returns data.frame with missing value in rownames*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19059>

            Bug ID: 19059
           Summary: as.data.frame.vector() returns data.frame with missing
                    value in rownames
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: minor
          Priority: P5
         Component: Misc
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:hpages.on.github@gmail.com|hpages.on.github@gmail.com>

Duplicates or missing values are in principle not allowed in the rownames of a
data.frame but for some reason `as.data.frame.vector()` only checks for
duplicates and not for missing values (see line 255 in
`src/library/base/dataframe.R`).

So for example, this seems to produce the intended result, which is to NOT
propagate the names on the supplied vector to the rownames of the returned
data.frame when they would lead to unacceptable rownames:

    > as.data.frame(setNames(21:24, c("a", "b", "b", "d")), optional=TRUE)
    1 21
    2 22
    3 23
    4 24

But in this example:

    > as.data.frame(setNames(21:24, c("a", "b", NA, "d")), optional=TRUE)   
    a    21
    b    22
    <NA> 23
    d    24

The NA made it to the rownames of the data.frame!

Note that trying to smuggle more than one NA to the rownames doesn't work:

    > as.data.frame(setNames(21:24, c("a", NA, NA, "d")), optional=TRUE)
    1 21
    2 22
    3 23
    4 24

but that's only because of the no-duplicates rule.

Also note that this issue in `as.data.frame.vector()` leads to the following
confusing behavior of the `data.frame()` constructor:

    ## This works:
    > data.frame(A=11:14, B=setNames(21:24, c("a", NA, NA, "d")))
       A  B
    1 11 21
    2 12 22
    3 13 23
    4 14 24

    ## but this doesn't:
    > data.frame(A=11:14, B=setNames(21:24, c("a", "b", NA, "d")))
    Error in data.frame(A = 11:14, B = setNames(21:24, c("a", "b", NA,  : 
      row names contain missing values

Fixing `as.data.frame.vector()` with an explicit check for NAs would make
`data.frame()` work (and return a data.frame with automatic rownames)
**whatever** the number of NAs in the names of the `B` variable.

Suggested fix is to replace the check on line 255 in
src/library/base/dataframe.R:

    if(length(row.names <- names(x)) != nrows || anyDuplicated(row.names))

with:

    if(length(row.names <- names(x)) != nrows || anyDuplicated(row.names) ||
anyNA(row.names))
